A Diameter of 3 Cm. The Transmission Counters Consisted
Of Four Closely Spaced Circular Counters with Gradually
Increasing Diameters,Each 5 mm Thick. Close to the Last
Counter a 50*50 Cm**2 Copper Plate of Variable
Thickness was Mounted,Followed by a Fifth Counter,45 Cm
In Diameter. At the Rear of the Telescope was Placed an
Efficiency Counter,3 mm Thick and 6 Cm in Diameter. All
Counters were Viewed by 56 Avp Photomultipliers. The
Telescope Assembly was Mounted on a Trolley Movable on
A Rail in the Beam Direction Thus Allowing a Variation
Of the Range of Solid Angles.
All Targets had Dimensions in the Plane Perpendicular
To the Beam Such That All Particles Passing Through
The Beam Defining Counters Traversed the Full Target
Lengths Even If They were Elastically Scattered Off a
